statement.focus_question: Is it true that $f(p)/p\to 0$ for almost all $p$?
statement.normalized: For any prime $p$, let $f(p)$ be the least integer such that $f(p)!+1\equiv 0\pmod{p}$. Is it true that there are infinitely many $p$ for which $f(p)=p-1$? Is it true that $f(p)/p\to 0$ for almost all $p$?
statement.original:
For any prime $p$, let $f(p)$ be the least integer such that $f(p)!+1\equiv 0\pmod{p}$.

Is it true that there are infinitely many $p$ for which $f(p)=p-1$?
Is it true that $f(p)/p\to 0$ for almost all $p$?
